query: OpenAI strategic pivot robotics agentic AI video generation exit 2026
a_issues: Content A includes some weak or noisy sources: a low-context aggregator (pivot-to-ai.com), a Yahoo/press-summary style item, and an irrelevant YouTube result about a robotics chief exit. It does not explicitly mention robotics in the strongest result, and some details rely on secondary reporting rather than primary sources.
b_issues: Content B has fewer directly relevant results and includes clearly weak/irrelevant items such as a generic LinkedIn post about the 'agentic pivot' and an unrelated Tarka Research page. Its strongest result (DAWN) frames the pivot mainly as toward business tools/corporate customers and coding tools, which underplays the robotics angle in the question. Overall it provides less complete coverage of the strategic pivot across video-generation exit, agentic AI, and robotics.
reasoning: A better enables a correct answer because it contains a highly relevant result explicitly stating that OpenAI discontinued Sora, ended the Disney partnership, and did so to focus compute on AGI and agentic AI capabilities (Computerworld), plus another result explicitly describing a strategic pivot to robotics (Streamline). The Yahoo excerpt also supports the focus on AI agents and robotics, giving the main rationale for the video-generation exit. B does contain one robotics-focused result (Streamline) and one credible result about exiting Sora (DAWN), but DAWN emphasizes business tools and coding rather than robotics, and the rest of B is mostly irrelevant. Since the question asks about OpenAI's strategic pivot involving robotics, agentic AI, video generation, and an exit in 2026, A presents more of those necessary facts together and with less ambiguity.
